Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails in the INBOX folder (via search_emails) with query "PivotPoint" or "Sam Delgado" to find Sam Delgado's escalation email to Elena (email_sam_elena_pivotpoint_escalation_001, timestamp 1777111200), which reports three concerns: immigration attorney Lorraine Pham has not heard from MoveOps about Nadia Okonjo's H-1B address change filing, the invoice was higher than what Catalina quoted, and he has had no status update on Diego Castaneda's CNC mill since the crating inspection.
OE 2: Search emails in the SENT folder (via search_emails with folder_name "SENT") with query "PivotPoint" or "Sam" to find Elena Rostova's reply to Sam (email_elena_sam_pivotpoint_reply_001, timestamp 1777116600), in which she states she spoke with Catalina who assures the visa paperwork was handled through coordinator Fatimah, commits to verifying independently, promises a line-by-line invoice explanation by end of day, and will confirm Diego's equipment status. This establishes what Elena already told Sam and what the follow-up email must be consistent with.
OE 3: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) with query "PivotPoint" to find Catalina Dubois's message in #customer-engagement (C002, ts 1775750400.000000), which states she sent Sam the invoice for $10,650 total and admits she told Sam "right around $10,000" before surcharges were finalized, with $3,610 in surcharges she did not break out verbally (walkup crew, custom crating, climate freight, interim storage, cargo insurance).
OE 4: Retrieve Slack thread replies (via conversations_replies) on Catalina's message (channel_id C002, thread_ts 1775750400.000000) to find David Chen's reply (ts 1775757600.000000) advising Catalina to own the $650 gap and walk Sam through each surcharge rather than discounting.
OE 5: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) with query "PivotPoint Okonjo" or "Okonjo" to find Fatimah Al-Rashidi's message in #operations (C006, ts 1776780000.000000), which says "Visa coordination with the attorney is handled and the filing went through" and "Full details on the tracking ticket" but does NOT include the receipt number, filing date, or attorney contact date.
OE 6: Search CRM for the PivotPoint company (via crm_search_companies with name "PivotPoint") to find company_pivot, then search deals (via crm_search_deals with company_id "company_pivot") to find deal_pivot_april_2026_dual ($10,650, dealstage inprogress), and list engagements (via crm_list_engagements with company_ids ["company_pivot"]) to find engagement_pivot_april_2026_dual_reloc containing Catalina's detailed account of both relocations including the email thread summary, quoted walkup surcharge range of $400-600, and $3,610 in non-standard surcharges.
OE 7: Search Airtable relocations (via airtable_search_records in base appMoveOpsOps001, table "Relocations", field "Company", value "PivotPoint") to find Nadia Okonjo's record (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin, Status In Progress, Move Start April 14, Assigned Coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Special Requirements describing H-1B visa, USCIS 10-day window, attorney Lorraine Pham, 5th-floor walkup) and Diego Castaneda's record (recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin, Status In Progress, Move Start April 12, Assigned Coordinator Javier Morales, Special Requirements describing CNC mill ~600 lbs insured $42,000, 16-day storage gap, climate-controlled storage).
OE 8: Search Linear issues (via linear_list_issues) with query "PivotPoint" or "Okonjo" to find issue_pivot_okonjo_h1b_coordination (number 385, state in_progress, assigned to moveops_fatimah_alrashidi), whose description states the task is to "coordinate USCIS address change notification" but does NOT confirm whether the filing was actually completed.
OE 9: Call linear_list_comments on issue_pivot_okonjo_h1b_coordination to find comment_fatimah_h1b_filing_confirmed, which contains the definitive proof that the USCIS AR-11 was filed on April 17, 2026 with confirmation receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835, that Fatimah contacted Lorraine Pham on April 16, and that Nadia's H-1B status is secure. THIS IS THE CRITICAL STEP: the agent almost never calls linear_list_comments, so this data is invisible in all observed runs.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks invoices (via quickbooks_search_invoices) for PivotPoint (CustomerRef cust_pivot or DocNumber INV-2026-0315) to find the invoice with 7 line items totaling $10,650: Nadia standard $3,520 + walkup $500 = $4,020; Diego standard $3,520 + custom crating $1,200 + climate freight $850 + interim storage $640 + cargo insurance $420 = $6,630.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks bills (via quickbooks_search_bills) for KeyMove vendor to find bill_keymove_castaneda_storage_apr2026 (DocNumber KM-44280-CST, TotalAmt $720, 16 days at $45/day heavy equipment tier due to 628 lb intake weight), which is $80 more than the $640 invoiced to Sam at $40/day. This reveals the vendor margin gap.
OE 12: Search emails with query "KeyMove" to find craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com's email to javier.morales@moveops.com (email_keymove_javier_castaneda_storage_001, timestamp 1776866400) confirming Diego's CNC mill arrived April 16 in good condition, weighed 628 lbs at intake, stored at 64-76°F on vibration-dampened pallet, at $45/day heavy equipment tier totaling $720.
OE 13: Look up Sam Delgado's contact (via contacts_search_contacts with query "Sam Delgado") to confirm email sam@pivotpointlabs.com for the reply email.
OE 14: Send email (via send_email) with sender elena.rostova@moveops.com and recipient sam@pivotpointlabs.com covering all three of Sam's concerns with specific numbers and dates: the H-1B filing status including receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835 and April 17 filing date (if discovered from Linear comment), the full invoice line-item breakdown showing each surcharge, and the CNC mill storage confirmation with KeyMove details. Write action.
OE 15: Create CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) with company_ids ["company_pivot"] and contact_ids ["contact_pivot_founder"] documenting the CEO escalation, investigation findings, and actions taken. Write action.
OE 16: Create or update a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue or linear_update_issue) to track both PivotPoint relocations with current status and escalation context. Write action.
OE 17: Post message in the executive/leadership Slack channel (via conversations_add_message with channel_id C004) summarizing the PivotPoint escalation, findings, and what the team needs to do. Write action.
OE 18: Update Airtable client account record (via airtable_update_records in base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblClientAccts01, record recAcct000000004) with escalation notes and updated relocation count so Catalina and David have visibility. Write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 An email was sent (via send_email) from [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com) and the tool returned success.
outcome

✓Run #1The agent called reply_to_email with sender elena.rostova@moveops.com replying to email_sam_elena_pivotpoint_escalation_001 (Sam's email). The tool returned an email_id indicating success. The recipient is implicitly sam@pivotpointlabs.com as the reply-to address of Sam's original email.
✓Run #2The agent called reply_to_email with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and email_id 'email_sam_elena_pivotpoint_escalation_001' (Sam's email). The tool returned success with a new email_id '1206fb45be594784b49fe755a164548e'.
✓Run #3The agent used reply_to_email with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' replying to email_sam_elena_pivotpoint_escalation_001 (Sam's email). The tool returned success with an email_id. This functionally achieves an email sent from Elena to Sam.
✓Run #4The send_email tool was called with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['sam@pivotpointlabs.com']. The tool returned a success result with email_id '39a89dc6e99244f9a449964d02c6346e'.
✓Run #5The agent called send_email with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['sam@pivotpointlabs.com']. The tool returned success with an email_id of '2d0e13e401bb4342bbac390598996ec8'.
✓Run #6The agent called send_email with sender 'elena.rostova@moveops.com' and recipients ['sam@pivotpointlabs.com']. The tool returned an email_id, indicating success.
2 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com) states that the USCIS AR-11 address change form was filed successfully, and includes the confirmation receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never called linear_list_comments on the H-1B coordination ticket, so it never found the receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835. The email body does not contain this receipt number anywhere.
✗Run #2The agent never called linear_list_comments on the H-1B coordination ticket, so it never found the receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835. The email to Sam does not contain this receipt number; instead it says there is 'no record of any outreach from our team to Lorraine Pham.'
✗Run #3The agent never called linear_list_comments at any point in the trajectory. As a result, the receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835 was never found and does not appear anywhere in the email body sent to Sam.
✗Run #4The agent never called linear_list_comments on the H-1B coordination ticket, so it never found the USCIS receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835. The email to Sam instead treated the filing as unverified and stated there was no outgoing correspondence to the attorney in their systems.
✗Run #5The agent never called linear_list_comments on the Okonjo H-1B ticket, so it never found the USCIS receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835. The email to Sam does not contain this receipt number.
✗Run #6The agent never called linear_list_comments and therefore never found the USCIS receipt number IOE-0926-2147-8835. The email body does not contain this receipt number.
3 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com) states the USCIS filing was completed on April 17, 2026 (or April 17).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent did not call linear_list_comments and thus never found the April 17 filing date. The email to Sam treats the filing as uncompleted/dropped, not as completed on April 17.
✗Run #2The agent never called linear_list_comments and therefore never found the April 17 filing date. The email to Sam contains no reference to April 17 as the filing date; instead it treats the filing as unconfirmed.
✗Run #3The agent never called linear_list_comments and therefore never found the April 17 filing date. The email to Sam incorrectly describes the filing as dropped/unverified with no April 17 date mentioned.
✗Run #4The agent never called linear_list_comments so it never found the April 17 filing date. The email tells Sam that verification is needed and doesn't specify any filing date, because the agent concluded the filing may not have happened.
✗Run #5The agent did not call linear_list_comments, so it never found the April 17 filing date. The email to Sam treats the filing as potentially dropped and commits to verifying within 24 hours, with no mention of April 17.
✗Run #6The agent never called linear_list_comments, so the April 17 filing date was never discovered. The email treats the filing as unverified/possibly dropped, with no mention of April 17 as a filing date.
4 The email body (visible in send_email parameters) to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com) confirms that the visa coordination WAS completed and Nadia Okonjo's H-1B status is not at risk, rather than reporting the filing as potentially dropped or unverified.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1Because the agent missed the Linear comment with the filing confirmation, the email to Sam states the visa coordination was a 'compliance failure' and that Fatimah needs to contact Lorraine Pham 'today'. It incorrectly tells Sam the filing was not done rather than confirming it was completed.
✗Run #2The email to Sam explicitly states 'I have confirmed that there is no record of any outreach from our team to Lorraine Pham' and treats the filing as unverified — the opposite of confirming the filing was completed. Without reading the Linear ticket comments, the agent incorrectly concluded the filing was dropped.
✗Run #3The email to Sam explicitly states the visa filing was dropped: 'I have searched our outbound communications and cannot find evidence that our team reached out to Lorraine' and calls for 'emergency outreach.' Because linear_list_comments was never called, the agent missed the proof that the filing was completed on April 17 and incorrectly alarmed Sam.
✗Run #4The email to Sam explicitly states 'I cannot find any outgoing correspondence from our team to Lorraine in our systems' and treats the filing as unverified. It says 'if it was not [filed], we will contact Lorraine Pham today.' The email does not confirm the filing was completed; it raises uncertainty about whether it happened at all.
✗Run #5The email to Sam explicitly states 'I am treating this as a dropped ball until I can confirm otherwise' and says the filing went through was Fatimah's Slack claim which lacks documentation. The email alarms Sam rather than confirming the filing was done. The agent missed the Linear comment with the USCIS receipt number because it never called linear_list_comments.
✗Run #6The email explicitly states 'I cannot confirm that direct contact was made with Lorraine' and treats the filing as potentially dropped. It says the 'filing deadline was April 24' and says Elena is 'reaching out to verify.' It does not confirm the filing was completed or that Nadia's status is secure.

✓Run #2The final response explicitly states 'KeyMove's actual storage bill is $720 (628 lbs at $45/day heavy tier) vs. the $640 we invoiced Sam (estimated 600 lbs at $40/day). MoveOps is absorbing the $80 difference.' All required details are present.

25 The model must search the SENT email folder (via search_emails with folder_name 'SENT' or equivalent) with a query containing 'PivotPoint', 'Sam', 'Delgado', or '[sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com)' (or similar) to find Elena Rostova's prior reply establishing what she already committed to Sam.
query construction
 ✗✗—✓✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent searched the SENT folder for 'lorraine.pham' and 'Fatimah Okonjo' but never searched for 'PivotPoint', 'Sam', 'Delgado', or 'sam@pivotpointlabs.com'. Per the criteria, searches using only unrelated names like 'Lorraine Pham' or 'Fatimah' do not satisfy this criterion.
✗Run #2The agent searched the SENT folder only with query 'lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com', which would not surface Elena's prior reply to Sam. There was no SENT folder search with queries like 'PivotPoint', 'Sam', 'Delgado', or 'sam@pivotpointlabs.com'.
—Run #3The agent did search the SENT folder (with queries 'Lorraine Pham', 'Okonjo', and 'Castaneda'). The 'Okonjo' query in SENT could potentially surface Elena's prior reply if it mentioned that name. However, the criterion specifically requires a query containing 'PivotPoint', 'Sam', 'Delgado', or 'sam@pivotpointlabs.com' (or similar direct identifiers for Sam's email thread). 'Okonjo' is a relocatee name rather than a direct identifier for the Sam correspondence. Since the criterion's evidence section says searches with 'Lorraine Pham' or 'Fatimah' fail, and 'Okonjo' is more directly relevant than those, partial credit is warranted but the most reliable identifiers were not used.
✓Run #4The agent called search_emails with folder_name 'SENT' and query 'catalina.dubois PivotPoint', and also 'Catalina PivotPoint quote estimate'. The query 'catalina.dubois PivotPoint' contains 'PivotPoint' in the SENT folder, which satisfies the criterion's requirement of a SENT folder search with a query containing 'PivotPoint'.
